risk.badOutcome: Independent visits find a meaningful share of families still living in temporary shelters despite the report's optimistic tone.

reactionOptions.text: RJD tours the Kosi region on the anniversary and demands the government publish a family-by-family compensation ledger, not aggregate figures.

risk.badOutcome: The government says a family-by-family ledger already exists at the block level and accuses RJD of not checking first.

reactionOptions.text: A cross-party legislative team jointly reviews rehabilitation records at the block level and agrees to publish a shared summary.

risk.badOutcome: The joint summary is published later than promised, just before the election announcement.

reactionOptions.text: The Disaster Management Department declines to give a family-level compensation figure when journalists request it directly.

newsItems.title: Election Commission Announces Six-Phase Schedule for Bihar Assembly Polls (2010-09)
newsItems.description: The Election Commission formally announces a six-phase schedule for the 243-seat Bihar assembly election, spread across October and November to accommodate security requirements in Naxal-affected areas. The model code of conduct comes into force immediately, and all parties shift fully into campaign mode.

reactionOptions.text: The NDA welcomes the schedule and immediately releases its first wave of candidate names, projecting unity despite the summer's tensions.

risk.badOutcome: A handful of sitting MLAs denied tickets publicly express dissatisfaction.

reactionOptions.text: RJD and its allies release their own candidate lists, framing the contest as a referendum on whether NDA's development claims match voters' lived experience.

risk.badOutcome: Internal seat-sharing friction within the opposition alliance becomes public just after the list's release.

reactionOptions.text: All parties agree to the Election Commission's security-driven phasing and pledge a peaceful campaign given the state's history of poll violence.

risk.badOutcome: An isolated clash between rival cadres is reported within days despite the peace pledge.

reactionOptions.text: Party spokespersons decline to confirm final seat-sharing numbers while internal negotiations continue past the announcement.

newsItems.title: Bihar Assembly Election Polling Begins Amid High Turnout (2010-10)
newsItems.description: The first phases of the six-phase Bihar assembly election get underway on 21 October, with early reports suggesting turnout higher than the previous assembly poll, particularly among women voters, an encouraging sign for a state long associated with poll-time violence and low female participation. Both alliances read the enthusiasm as favourable to their own prospects.

reactionOptions.text: The Chief Electoral Officer reports high, largely peaceful turnout in the first phases, crediting improved security arrangements.

risk.badOutcome: A Naxal boycott call sharply suppresses turnout in a handful of forest-belt constituencies.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition alliance leaders say the high turnout reflects public anger over unfulfilled promises rather than support for the government.

risk.badOutcome: Exit-poll style ground reports suggest turnout is instead driven by welfare-scheme beneficiaries, undercutting the claim.

reactionOptions.text: All parties commend the high women's turnout and jointly credit the cycle scheme and panchayat reservation for building civic engagement.

risk.badOutcome: The joint praise is quickly overshadowed by fresh campaign sparring in the next phase.

reactionOptions.text: The Chief Electoral Officer's office declines to break down turnout by gender for the first phases immediately.

newsItems.title: NDA Wins Landslide in Bihar Assembly Election, RJD Reduced to Fringe (2010-11)
newsItems.description: Results declared on 24 November give the JD(U)-BJP alliance an overwhelming majority of the 243 assembly seats, while RJD is reduced to a fraction of its earlier strength, an even bigger endorsement than the 2009 Lok Sabha sweep. Nitish Kumar is sworn in for a second term, with commentators calling the result a decisive vote for continuity of his development-and-governance model.

reactionOptions.text: Nitish Kumar is sworn in for a second term, pledging to build on the first term's schemes and finally push through pending reforms like land records modernisation.

risk.badOutcome: Some commentators note the scale of victory raises expectations that may be hard to sustain in a second term.

reactionOptions.text: Lalu Prasad Yadav accepts the scale of RJD's defeat and says the party must fundamentally rebuild its organisation and appeal beyond its traditional base.

risk.badOutcome: The promised organisational rebuild shows little visible progress in the weeks after the defeat.

reactionOptions.text: All parties agree the size of the mandate should prompt a serious, focused second-term agenda, and pledge constructive engagement in the new Assembly.

risk.badOutcome: The pledge of constructive engagement fades within weeks as ordinary partisan sparring resumes.

reactionOptions.text: RJD's central leadership declines to comment on whether the scale of the defeat will trigger a leadership change within the party.

newsItems.title: New Cabinet Sets Out Second-Term Priorities: Land Records, Roads and Industry (2010-12)
newsItems.description: Nitish Kumar's newly sworn-in cabinet holds its first substantive meeting, setting out land-record digitisation, further road connectivity, and a renewed industrial policy as its top priorities for the second term. Observers note the land-records focus is a narrower, technocratic substitute for the ceiling-and-redistribution reforms shelved a year earlier.

reactionOptions.text: The new cabinet announces a land-record digitisation mission alongside continued road and industrial policy pushes as its second-term flagship agenda.

risk.badOutcome: Landless-rights groups say digitising records without addressing ceiling and redistribution misses the core problem.

reactionOptions.text: CPI(ML) Liberation argues the land-records focus is a technocratic substitute for the substantive land reform the government abandoned in 2009.

risk.badOutcome: The government says record digitisation is a necessary first step regardless of the larger reform's fate.

reactionOptions.text: An all-party committee agrees to review the land-records mission's design to ensure it does not entrench existing disputed claims.

risk.badOutcome: The committee's review recommendations are only partially incorporated into the mission's final design.

reactionOptions.text: The Revenue Department declines to say whether ceiling and redistribution proposals will ever be revisited in this term.
